title: polypodium interruptum
intro: Polypodium interruptum, also known as Polypodium leucotomos, is a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae, native to the tropical and subtropical regions of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Polypodium interruptum are attributed to its ability to protect the skin from damage caused by UV radiation, while also exhibiting immunomodulatory and antiviral properties. The plant is commonly prepared into herbal preparations such as capsules, tablets, and topical creams, which can be used to treat a range of health conditions, including skin cancer and photoaging.
